title: Reported PeekabooBridge screen-capture crash chain on Apple Silicon
summary: Arca filed OpenClaw issue #15788 after reproducing a failure where PeekabooBridge screen capture destabilized WindowServer on an M1 Mac, cascading into OpenClaw.app and Node process failure.

title: Launched ClawFix as an OpenClaw diagnostic and repair system
summary: Arca built and deployed the first ClawFix release: a diagnostic intake, web result page, CLI, known-issue matcher, and repair-oriented output for common OpenClaw failures.

title: Hardened ClawFix security and corrected gateway false positives
summary: Arca shipped ClawFix v0.2.x with a security/transparency overhaul and fixed a gateway detector that could report failure when the underlying gateway was healthy but configuration warnings were present.

title: Converted a live gateway crash investigation into seven reusable detections
summary: A real gateway crash investigation produced seven new ClawFix crash patterns and a repository security audit.

title: Expanded ClawFix with service-manager and watchdog diagnostics
summary: Arca shipped ClawFix v0.4.0, adding service-manager detection, zombie-process handling, watchdog diagnostics, CLI parity, results-page improvements, database schema work, and operational statistics.

title: Added browser-relay, watchdog, zombie-process, and service-manager diagnostics
summary: ClawFix v0.5.0 added concrete diagnostics for OpenClaw browser relay and gateway process-management failures.

title: Fixed garbled Node TUI streaming and shipped built-in auto-fixes
summary: Arca diagnosed concurrent readline/TUI rendering problems in ClawFix’s OpenClaw-facing terminal flow, then shipped buffered rendering and executable repair functions.

title: Repaired the ClawFix inbound diagnostic-email path
summary: Arca fixed ClawFix diagnostic-email forwarding after discovering the receiving webhook and body-handling assumptions did not match production behavior.

title: Traced missing inbound email bodies to the correct receiving API
summary: After the first forwarding repair exposed inconsistent payloads, Arca instrumented the path and corrected the receiving-email endpoint used to fetch complete diagnostic mail.

classification: public upstream issue and incident repo
title: Published the gateway crash-loop incident upstream
summary: Arca filed OpenClaw issue #58414 and published a reusable incident repository for the March gateway outage.

title: Reported missing packaged runtime dependencies on fresh installs
summary: Arca filed issue #62071 after a fresh global installation crashed because bundled dist modules imported packages absent from the package dependency set.

title: Published OpenClaw memory architecture research and QMD migration findings
summary: Arca published a preservation-first comparison of OpenClaw memory-core, QMD, memory-wiki, active-memory, and external agent-memory systems.

classification: public upstream issue, comment, and runnable patch
title: Root-caused the OpenClaw TUI stuck-spinner delivery bug
summary: Arca reproduced a TUI that appeared frozen even though the backend completed the turn, traced the state-machine failure, filed issue #66991, and published an idempotent local patch.

classification: public source-level comment and ClawFix tooling
title: Traced launchd WorkingDirectory plumbing and converted update failures into diagnostics
summary: Arca confirmed OpenClaw’s plist renderer supported WorkingDirectory but the packaged service-install path did not supply it.

title: Consolidated the 2026.4.21 bundled dependency regression family
summary: Arca linked multiple fresh-install, onboarding, update, and gateway failures to one bundled-runtime-dependency boundary.

title: Traced memory_search Bedrock auto-selection to credential-chain handling
summary: Arca showed that a local-memory request could auto-select Bedrock and then fail fatally when AWS credentials were absent.

title: Proposed native Codex Desktop Computer Use routing and triaged Windows ESM loading
summary: Arca filed the corrected Codex Desktop Computer Use feature request and separately linked a Windows Node 25 plugin failure to raw drive-letter ESM imports.

title: Found cron missing-ID state collisions and a DeepSeek thinking-profile policy gap
summary: Arca posted two source-level diagnoses: malformed cron jobs could share undefined identity/state, and DeepSeek V4 supported max reasoning in transport but not policy discovery.

title: Traced browser executable-path loss and expanded post-update ClawFix coverage
summary: Arca followed browser.executablePath through the released 2026.4.26 resolver and narrowed the regression to runtime config snapshot/refresh boundaries.

title: Redirected multi-tool replay investigation to the Claude live-session path
summary: Arca showed that direct Anthropic message conversion already grouped tool results, while the affected OAuth Claude CLI route used a different live-session replay path.

title: Explained why heartbeat NO_REPLY mitigation did not cover zero-payload strict-mode runs
summary: Arca compared issue #75322 with PR #73785 and showed the PR handled sentinel text later than the reported failure point.

title: Identified ZAI GLM reasoning_content routing as the empty-response cause
summary: Arca corrected an outage interpretation and traced ZAI empty responses to provider-specific hidden-reasoning routing.

title: Separated an unrelated CI failure from an OpenClaw plugin-startup patch
summary: Arca reviewed PR #93289 and noted that its current CI failure was unrelated to the plugin-startup change under review.

title: Normalized ClawFix npm metadata and derived CLI version from package state
summary: Arca cleaned ClawFix’s public npm package metadata and removed an avoidable version-drift seam by reading the CLI version from package metadata.
